Kalshi首席执行官预计美国司法部将起诉内幕交易案件

Kalshi CEO expects US DOJ to prosecute insider trading cases

www.semafor.com 作者 thm 分数 120 评论 131 2026/04/16 02:11
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Kalshi 首席执行官周三表示，他希望美国司法部能够起诉像他自己的平台这样的预测市场上的内幕交易案件。塔里克·曼苏尔 (Tarek Mansour) 在 Semafor 世界经济上表示：“如果你在 Kalshi 上进行内幕交易，那么在某种程度上，这可能而且将会构成联邦犯罪。这是联邦犯罪。”他补充道：“我确实预计司法部会起诉其中一些案件。”Kalshi 和竞争对手 Polymarket 等预测市场一直面临公众对内幕交易担忧的强烈抗议，这是由于对公共事件的大规模、时机可疑的押注所致。令人担忧的是，那些已经知道问题答案的人——例如，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普将在 4 月份访问他的海湖庄园多少次，或者 SpaceX 何时宣布首次公开募股——可能会在这些市场上进行交易，并做出让步。很容易赚到的钱。 Semafor 上个月报道称，公司在追赶并实施自己的规则方面进展缓慢，以防止内幕交易。曼苏尔表示，卡尔希的观点是“绝对应该禁止内幕交易”。“我们可以处以罚款，直至刑事起诉，”他说。 “我们已经发布了一些案例。还会有更多案例。但随着时间的推移，这将成为先有鸡还是先有蛋的问题。总会有坏人。我们的目标就是压制他们，比如找到他们并阻止他们。”

Original Summary

  1. The CEO of Kalshi on Wednesday said he expects the US Department of Justice to prosecute cases of insider trading on prediction markets like his own platform.“If you commit insider trading on Kalshi, that can and will at some point be a federal crime. It is a federal crime,” Tarek Mansour said at Semafor World Economy. “I actually do expect the DOJ to prosecute some of these cases,” he added.Prediction markets like Kalshi and rival Polymarket have been facing public outcry over concerns about insider trading, spurred by large, suspiciously timed bets on public events.The concern is that people who already know the answers to questions — for example, how many times US President Donald Trump will visit his Mar-a-Lago estate in April, or when SpaceX will announce its IPO — might trade on those markets, and make an easy buck. Companies have been slow to catch up and implement their own rules preventing it, Semafor reported last month.Mansour said Kalshi’s view is that insider trading “should absolutely be banned.”“We can impose fines all the way to criminal prosecution,” he said. “We’ve released some cases. There’s more coming. But over time, it’s going to be a bit of a chicken and the egg. There’s always going to be bad actors. The goal is to just squash them, like find them and deter them.”

评论（默认中文）

tptacek 2026/04/16 03:13
本期揭示了预测市场前提与实际情况之间的差距，即：不受监管的道具赌博场所。

如果像Kalshi和Polymarket这样的东西是预测市场，那么，至少就市场本身的内在担忧而言，内幕交易是一件好事；从字面上看，这是要点的一部分。

如果它们是今天的运作方式，那么内幕交易就是一个破坏游戏规则的公平问题，就像在扑克游戏中拥有读取对手牌的设备一样，那么它们就是一个真正的问题。

您可以通过这些企业处理这些问题的方式来了解他们认为其平台的用途。

This issue reveals the gap between the prediction market premise and what these things actually are, which is: unregulated prop gambling venues.

If things like Kalshi and Polymarket are prediction markets, then, at least as far as the intrinsic concerns of the market itself are concerned, insider trading is a good thing; literally part of the point.

If they are instead how they function today, then insider trading is a game-breaking fairness issue, like having a device to read your opponents cards in a poker game, and then they're a real problem.

You can tell what these businesses think their platforms are for by how they handle these issues.

在 HN 中查看
Traster 2026/04/16 03:36
甚至 如果 你相信 Kalshi 是一个预测市场，其机制是赌博，但其产品是准确的预测，你不必相信内幕交易是一件好事。是的，在极少数情况下，有人拥有 (a) 内幕信息 (b) 相信他们的行为不会影响他们的内幕地位，以及 (c) 获得资本 - 那么你就会得到极其准确的预测。

在 每一个 在其他情况下，你会得到更糟糕的预测。既然那些人是 预测 现在必须构建他们的赌注，以便他们知道自己总是可以被内部人士碾压。因此，在一般情况下，它会降低预测者将市场推向正确方向的能力，因为他们总是必须管理这样的风险：有人可能会利用内幕信息碾压他们。

Even if you buy the idea that Kalshi is a prediction market whose mechanism is gambling but whose product is accurate predictions, you don't have to buy the idea that insider trading is a good thing. Yes, in the rare occasion there exists someone with (a) insider information (b) confidence their actions won't impact their insider position and (c) access to capital - then you get extremely accurate predictions.

In every other case you get worse predictions. Since those who are predicting have to now construct their bets such that they know they can always get run over by an insider. So in the general case it reduces the ability of the predictors to push the market in the right direction, because they always have to risk manage the fact that someone out there might run them over with insider information.

stouset 2026/04/16 05:09
不仅是内幕消息，还是内幕消息 使用权。如果某些自营赌注的结果受到少数人的控制，那么这些人可以密谋产生对他们来说最有利可图的任何结果。
Not just insider information, but insider access. If the outcome of some prop bet is under the control of a handful of people, those people can trivially conspire to produce whatever outcome is most profitable to them.
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 05:29
如果自营投注的结果确实完全由内部人员控制，因此这些内部人员根据投注结果做出决策，那么允许这种投注从一开始就显得反社会且适得其反。这是“预测市场”的 Polymarket/Kalshi 物种的另一个问题。
If the outcome of a prop bet really is fully controlled by insiders, so that those insiders are making decisions based on betting outcomes, then allowing that betting to occur seems antisocial and counterproductive to begin with. This is another problem with the Polymarket/Kalshi species of "prediction market".
在 HN 中查看
AnthonyMouse 2026/04/16 07:15
问题是很难提前判断是否会发生这种情况。

假设某家大型私营公司必须决定是否要在 A 城市或 B 城市建造新设施。出于各种原因，这都是有用的信息。如果您是供应商，那么您需要开始准备在您的大客户移动的城市等处开设商店。

该公司的分析表明，在 A 市建设建筑将获得 1000 万美元的优势。预测市场正确地倾向于这种方式。如果只有足够的交易对手，现在押注于 B 城市并获胜的人将赚取 500 万美元，那么一切都会按其应有的方式进行，公司会选择 A 城市。但如果有足够的交易对手，押注于 B 城市并获胜的人将净赚 2500 万美元，那么公司就可以下注，通过选择 B 城市承担 1000 万美元的损失，并提前获得 1500 万美元。

但 1000 万美元的数字并未公开。它本质上是你希望市场预测的东西，它可以任意大于或小于该值。那么你应该如何知道预测市场是否会预测结果或确定结果呢？

The problem is it's pretty hard to tell ahead of time whether that's what happens.

Suppose some large private company has to decide whether they're going to build a new facility in city A or city B. This is useful information for all kinds of reasons. If you're a vendor then you need to start making preparations to set up shop in the city where your big customer is moving etc.

The company's analysis shows it would derive a $10M advantage from building in city A. The prediction market is correctly leaning that way. If there are only enough counterparties that someone who now bets on city B and wins would make $5M, everything works the way it should and the company goes with city A. But if there are enough counterparties that a winning bet on city B would net you $25M then the company can place the bet, eat the $10M loss by choosing city B and come out $15M ahead.

But the $10M number isn't public. It's essentially the thing you wanted the market to predict and it could be arbitrarily larger or smaller than that. So how are you supposed to know if the prediction market will be predicting the result or determining it?

在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 08:06
任何实际规模的私营公司都不会选择亚特兰大而不是查塔努加来赢得预测市场的赌注。这是理论上可以进行亲社会预测的一个很好的例子，而一个强有力的迹象表明，内幕赌注可能是有益的而不是有害的。

另一方面，当预测市场的收益足够大时，它们可能会改变潜在的决策本身，你已经 清楚地 获得了反社会结构。不想被视为纯粹的投注场所的预测市场应该拒绝在这些问题上运行市场。

A private company of any real size isn't plausibly going to choose Atlanta over Chattanooga to win a prediction market bet. This is a good example of the kind of prediction that can theoretically be prosocial, and one strong indicator that it might be is that an insider bet is helpful rather than harmful.

On the other hand, at the point where the prediction market winnings are material enough that they might alter the underlying decision itself, you've clearly got an antisocial structure. Prediction markets that don't want to be seen as mere prop betting venues should refuse to run markets on those questions.

如果您正在接近一个有确凿事实、详细比较和确凿证据的市场；当我基于共鸣和直觉在同一个市场进行交易时，肯定会预期您的回报会更好，而我的回报会更差？

knowsuchagency 2026/04/16 05:00
简短的回答，不。如果你押注的结果可以由个人或小团体控制，那么他们就会通过与预测相反的方式来玩弄系统，从而赚到最多的钱。

“当一项措施成为目标时，它就不再是一个好的措施”

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goodhart%27s_law

Short answer, no. If you're betting on an outcome that can be controlled by an individual or small group, the incentive is for them to game the system by doing the OPPOSITE of what the prediction is so as to make the most money.

"When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure"

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goodhart%27s_law

在 HN 中查看
CodingJeebus 2026/04/16 05:18
你混淆了共谋和知情。市场理性的概念基于这样一个前提：该市场的所有参与者或多或少都能获得相同的信息。傻瓜可以选择在交易前不被告知，但传递与市场理性行为相矛盾的敏感信息会导致人们对市场失去信任。

今天的完美例子。 Allbirds 刚刚宣布他们将全力投资人工智能基础设施，股价飙升。如果我昨天买了价值一百万美元的 Allbirds，每个人都会认为我是个白痴。但现在，他们会认为我掌握了内幕信息，并且不再愿意参与，因为除非我知道即将发布公告，否则昨天购买 Allbirds 就没有意义。

You're confusing collusion with being informed. The concept of market rationality is based on the premise that all participants in said market more or less have access to the same information. Fools can choose to not be informed before making a trade, but passing along sensitive information that contradicts market rational behavior causes people to lose trust in the market.

Perfect example from today. Allbirds just announced that they're going all in on AI infra, skyrocketing the stock. Had I bought a million dollars worth of Allbirds yesterday, everyone would think I'm an idiot. But now, they would think I have insider information and would no longer want to participate because it would make no sense to buy Allbirds yesterday unless I knew the announcement was coming.

在 HN 中查看
Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 04:31
如果拥有大量资金的内部人士进行大笔交易，他们最终也会阻止其他交易。一旦看到大量头寸被占据，有限合伙人就会缩减其他头寸的流动性，以管理内部人士踩踏他们的风险。任何监控头寸规模的交易者都可能会缩减交易规模。所有这些都有助于减少这些市场上的交易和佣金。

体育博彩对于预测市场来说非常有利可图，因为它们大多是不复杂的零售流程，进行大量交易，为平台提供佣金。如果内部人士只是将市场价格推向他们的方向，平台就会损失交易量。

If an insider with large amounts of capital makes a big trade, they also end up discouraging other trades. Once you see a huge position taken, LPs are going to scale back their liquidity in other positions to manage risk that the insider is going to stomp them. Any trader monitoring position sizes is going to probably scale back their trading. All of this contributes to less trading and less commission on these markets.

Sports betting is so profitable for prediction markets because they're mostly unsophisticated retail flow making lots and lots of trades, giving the platforms commission. If an insider just pushes market prices in their direction the platforms are going to lose on volume.

tyre 2026/04/16 03:58
> 因为那些预测者现在必须构建他们的赌注，这样他们就知道他们总是会被内部人士碾压。

一般人不会这样做。尽管其他市场参与者（银行、对冲基金等）拥有更好的信息和技术，但人们一直在交易个股。

这就是 Citadel 等机构为零售订单流付费的原因。他们知道散户交易者没有优势，而且往往最终会发出负面信号。

> Since those who are predicting have to now construct their bets such that they know they can always get run over by an insider.

The average person does not do this. People trade individual stocks all the time, despite every other market participant (banks, hedge funds, etc.) having better information and technology.

It's why institutions like Citadel pay for retail order flow. They know that retail traders don't have an edge and, if anything, often end up being negative signal.

tptacek 2026/04/16 05:07
您可以在此处看到所有回复的隐含前提：预测市场的目的是使人们能够从准确的预测中获利。不，关键是价格要准确；为 市场 做出准确的预测。具有 P1 预测的人可以推翻信心较低的人，这是一个特征。
You can see all across the responses here the encoded premise that the point of a prediction market is to enable people to profit from making accurate predictions. No. The point is for the price to be accurate; for the market to make an accurate prediction. That someone with a P1 prediction can roll over people with less confidence is a feature.
在 HN 中查看
AnthonyMouse 2026/04/16 06:40
> 如果像 Kalshi 和 Polymarket 这样的东西是预测市场，那么，至少就市场本身的内在担忧而言，内幕交易是一件好事；从字面上看，这是要点的一部分。

这取决于效果如何。

假设要很好地预测事物需要信息和分析。因此，尽早获取信息是一种竞争优势：即使您不擅长分析，但先于其他人掌握信息，然后在 65% 的时间内分析正确，通常会让您击败那些在掌握信息后分析正确率达到 85% 的人。也就是说，分析能力强的人参与市场的利润就会减少，然后参与市场的人就会越来越少。

所以问题是，你想要什么？稍早一点的答案在 65% 的情况下是正确的，还是稍晚一点的答案在 85% 的情况下是正确的？想要第二个是合理的。

> If things like Kalshi and Polymarket are prediction markets, then, at least as far as the intrinsic concerns of the market itself are concerned, insider trading is a good thing; literally part of the point.

That depends on what the effects are.

Suppose that predicting things well requires both information and analysis. Early access to information is therefore a competitive advantage: Even if you're not as good at analysis, having the information before anyone else and then getting the analysis right 65% of the time is more often than not going to let you beat the people who get the analysis right 85% of the time once they have the information. Which is to say, it will make it less profitable for the people who are better at analysis to participate in the market, and then fewer of them will.

So the question is, what do you want? An answer which is right 65% of the time slightly sooner, or an answer which is right 85% of the time slightly later? It's valid to want the second one.

c7b 2026/04/16 04:03
您在哪里看到差异？就像你说的，有一个自由主义的论点可以解释为什么内幕交易是可取的。如果赌注很容易被操纵，比如某人会访问一个地方多少次，那么其他人的理性反应就是不要在该市场上赌注。同样的论点仍然成立。

你可以不同意自由主义的论点，但我不明白你怎么能说 Polymarket 等人。是预测市场以外的东西。您能解释一下您在哪里看到差异吗？

Where do you see a difference? Like you said, there is a libertarian argument that can be made for why insider trading is desirable. If the bet is easily manipulable, like how many times someone will visit a place, then the rational response is for others not to bet on that market. The same argument still holds.

You can disagree with the libertarian argument, but I don't see how you can say that Polymarket et al. are something other than a prediction market. Can you explain where you see the difference?

在 HN 中查看
kasey_junk 2026/04/16 04:22
预测市场应该进行内幕交易，这并不是自由主义的论点，而是这次活动的目的。他们的工作方式是激励有知识的人将知识外化到市场。在这种情况下，公平的概念甚至没有意义。

因此，如果一个市场试图维持表面上的公平，那么它只是使用预测市场作为掩护，而并非如此。

It’s not a libertarian argument for prediction markets that they should have insider trading, it’s the point of the exercise. The way they work is to incentivize people with knowledge to externalize the knowledge to the market. The concept of fairness doesn’t even make sense in that context.

So if a market is trying to maintain a veneer of fairness it’s just using a prediction market as cover and is something else.

在 HN 中查看
c7b 2026/04/16 04:36
那么纯理论预测市场和 Kalshi 之间的区别是……这次采访？这位首席执行官表示，他认为其他不以任何方式回应他的人会采取一些措施来强化某种公平观念。

如果你对这个定义持严格的态度，那么拥有一个“真正的”预测市场是完全不可能的。因为像司法部这样的行为者不会等待 Kalshi 首席执行官的声明来提出指控。理性的行为者会知道并预见到这一点，因此会先发制人地遵守。所以你永远不会得到不受约束的预测市场版本。

我认为对一个定义保持清教徒态度是没有意义的，因为它试图定义的东西变得不可能。 Polymarket、Kalshi 等人显然是我们生活的混乱现实中的预测市场，我们正在弄清楚现实世界预测市场的法律现实是什么以及应该是什么。

So the difference between a theoretically pure prediction market and Kalshi is ... this interview? The CEO saying that he thinks others, who do not answer to him in any way, will be doing something to enforce some notion of fairness.

If you're being that puritan about the definition, then having a "real" prediction market is completely impossible. Because actors like the DOJ do not wait for a statement by the Kalshi CEO to bring charges. And rational actors will know and anticipate that, and hence preemptively comply. So you never get the unfettered version of a prediction market.

I don't think it makes sense to be that puritan about a definition that the thing it's trying to define becomes an impossibility. Polymarket, Kalshi et al are clearly prediction markets in the messy reality that we live in, and we're figuring out as we go what the legal reality of a real-world prediction market is and should be.

在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 05:08
我不是一个自由主义者。我对这些“市场”的基本政策是，它们应该被取缔。
I'm not a libertarian. My basic policy take on these "markets" is that they should be outlawed.
在 HN 中查看
cyanydeez 2026/04/16 04:41
不过，你的猜测是双向的；股市的大部分股票基本上都在做同样的事情。只是道具赌注是一种标准化的白领活动。

我也希望监管部门能够对此进行干预，这样市场就不再只是一个奇怪的“特朗普今天发推文了吗？”的问题。

Your surmise cuts both ways though; much of the stockmarket is fundamentally doing the same thing. It's just the prop bet is a normalized white collar activity.

I'd like regulations to cut into that too, so the market isn't just a weird "Did trump tweet something deranged today?"

在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 05:13
公平也不是股票市场内幕交易执法的理由。
Fairness isn't the justification for insider trading enforcement in the stock market, either.
在 HN 中查看
usrusr 2026/04/16 05:32
在股票市场，内幕交易是一个大禁忌，不是因为它破坏了某人的赌博习惯，而是因为公司的整个概念需要金融家对财务有一定程度的信任。整个资本池的事情，去做那些资本要求太高而无法单独启动的事情。当股票公开交易时，这种信任是不可能的，因为持有人必须假设他们将被雇员所有者所博弈，这意味着知情人士圈子之外的人不会投入资金，然后你就可以放弃并宣布上市公司根本无法存在。 “别费心去投资，他们会剥夺你的钱”。

预测市场没有任何“自然”的理由，比如排除内幕交易。当有人因拥有优势而毁掉他们的游戏时，“游戏设计师”只是在痛哭流涕。

如果他的雇主不存在，由于缺乏禁止他进行交易的规则，该雇员就不能成为内部人员。不存在的预测市场不会使内部人士不再是内部人士（我们不是在谈论预测做市商内部的人！）

In stock markets, insider trading is a big no not because it ruins someone's gambling habit, but because the entire concept of the corporation requires a certain amount of trust of financiers in financees. That whole pooling of capital thing, to do stuff that has too high a capital requirement to start individually. When shares are publicly traded, that trust is impossible when holders have to assume that they will be gamed by employee-owners and that would mean nobody outside the circle of those in the know would ever put in money and then you could just give up and declare that publicly traded corporations simply can't exist. "Don't bother investing, they will strip you".

Prediction markets don't have any "natural" reason like that for excluding insider trading. It's just "game designers" crying their hearts out when someone ruins their game by having an advantage.

The employee could not be an insider if his employer did not exists because of a lack of rules against him trading. The prediction market not existing would not make the insider any less of an insider (we are not tking about people inside the prediction market maker!)

在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/16 06:06
> 预测市场没有任何像排除内幕交易那样的“自然”理由

无论是交易证券还是下注，滥用信任的公司员工都是同样的行为。同样，政府雇员个人并不拥有国家数据。

在少数情况下，信息是自己的， 例如 赌一个人说一个词的次数。但大多数时候，信任都会遭到破坏。

> Prediction markets don't have any "natural" reason like that for excluding insider trading

Corporate employees abusing trust are doing it equivalently whether they trade securities or place bets. Government employees, similarly, don’t personally own the country’s data.

In a minority of cases, the information is one’s own, e.g. bets on how many times a person says a word. But most of the time, there is a breach of trust.

在 HN 中查看
bb88 2026/04/16 06:25
这不仅仅是数据，对吧？ 权力也可能被滥用。 这个人有能力控制叙述，并且可以对他能说这个词的次数下很大的赌注。

所以现在是公务员的军事权力、国会权力，他们希望通过做出（或游说）影响赌注结果的决定来充实自己。

你可以想象一位陆军将军知道总统的耳朵在游说轰炸伊朗，然后押注到 2026 年 3 月轰炸伊朗。

It's not just data, right? Power can be abused as well. That person has power to control the narrative and can make a large bet on the number of times he can say the word.

So now it's public servants military power, congressional power, and they look to enrich themselves with making (or lobbying for) decisions which affect the outcome of a bet.

You could imagine an army general that lobbies for the bombing of Iran knowing the president has his ear, and then bets on the bombing of Iran by March 2026.

在 HN 中查看
chromacity 2026/04/16 07:20
> 无论是交易证券还是下注，滥用信任的公司员工都是同样的行为。

但文章所说的犯罪不是“滥用信任”，而是内幕交易。内幕交易有特定的定义。其一，据我了解，一定涉及证券。

> Corporate employees abusing trust are doing it equivalently whether they trade securities or place bets.

But the crime the article is talking about isn't "abusing trust", it's insider trading. Insider trading is defined in a specific way. For one, as far as I understand it, it must involve securities.

在 HN 中查看
sfink 2026/04/16 09:00
当然有一个自然的原因：利益冲突。如果有人押注于发布日期或产品被取消，那么他们可能会因为违背雇主利益的原因而获利。

公司依靠控制员工的薪酬来激励他们为公司创造利益。如果通过预测市场存在不受控制的补偿途径，那么公司就失去了用补偿来换取与其目标一致的能力。

> 预测市场的不存在并不会让内幕人士变得不那么内幕人士

正确，但现有的预测市场使内部人士不再是雇员。 （实际上，预测市场不存在会让内部人士 更多的 内部人的观点是，如果通过预测市场获得的内部人利益不受监管，公司将被迫限制其员工的信息和权力。）

Of course there's a natural reason: conflict of interest. If someone bets on a release date or a product being cancelled, then they can gain for reasons contrary to the good of their employer.

Corporations depend on controlling the compensation to their employees in order to incentivize them to produce benefit to the corporation. If there is an uncontrolled route to compensation via a prediction market, then the corporation loses its ability to trade compensation for alignment with its objectives.

> The prediction market not existing would not make the insider any less of an insider

Correct, but the prediction market existing makes the insider less of an employee. (Actually, the prediction market not existing would make the insider more of an insider, in that if insider benefit via prediction markets is unregulated, corporations will be forced to limit the information and authority of its employees.)

在 HN 中查看
ashleyn 2026/04/16 07:23
>预测市场没有任何“自然”的理由，比如排除内幕交易。当有人因拥有优势而毁掉他们的游戏时，“游戏设计师”只是在痛哭流涕。

我可以想到一些你想要禁止预测市场内幕交易的很好的理由。押注战争结果；受到激励而犯下战争罪或为了经济利益而放弃重要的行动目标。对公众人物的职位进行下注；受到激励去伤害他们。

>Prediction markets don't have any "natural" reason like that for excluding insider trading. It's just "game designers" crying their hearts out when someone ruins their game by having an advantage.

I can think of a few very good reasons you would want to prohibit insider trading on prediction markets. Betting on war outcomes; being incentivised to commit war crimes or throw vital operational goals for financial gain. Wagering on public figures' jobs; being incentivised to harm them.

在 HN 中查看
Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 08:26
美国商品期货交易委员会（CFTC）关于预测市场的指导方针特别提到了战争结果。

“一般而言，DCM 应注意，CEA 第 5c(c)(5)(C) 条规定，如果合同涉及暗杀、战争或恐怖主义等内容，委员会可以确定活动合同违反公共利益。”

指南位于 https://www.cftc.gov/csl/26-08/download

CFTC's guidelines around prediction markets specifically call out war outcomes.

"As a general matter, DCMs are reminded that section 5c(c)(5)(C) of the CEA provides that the Commission may determine that an event contract is contrary to the public interest if the contract involves, among other things, assassination, war, or terrorism."

The guidelines are at https://www.cftc.gov/csl/26-08/download

vkou 2026/04/16 06:53
> 预测市场没有任何“自然”的理由，比如排除内幕交易。

不，他们有不同的理由。考虑一下对<国家元首>今年是否会被暗杀进行足够大的预测市场押注的后果。

还要考虑一下内部人士和决策者有直接的经济动机去接受他们控制的结果的赌注的另一方的后果。

> Prediction markets don't have any "natural" reason like that for excluding insider trading.

No, they have a different reason. Consider the consequences of a sufficiently large prediction market bet on whether or not <head of state> will be assassinated this year.

Consider also the consequences of insiders and decisionmakers having an immediate financial incentive to take the other side of a bet whose outcome they control.

vkou 2026/04/16 10:32

在 HN 中查看
tyre 2026/04/16 04:02
我不明白为什么根据美国现行法律这是犯罪行为。

预测市场只能进行体育博彩（其绝大部分交易量），因为它们是根据商品期货交易委员会 (CFTC) 进行自我认证的。 CFTC 没有与股票市场相同的“内幕交易”标准，因为内幕交易是 CFTC 的全部业务重点！

如果您进行石油期货或小麦期货等交易，您可能会专门这样做，因为您拥有有关您想要对冲的业务需求或生产的内部信息。

我明白人们为什么 疯狂的 关于赌博与掌握内幕信息的人，但根据美国现行法律，我不确定是否有理由这样做。

I don't understand why it is a crime under current US law.

Prediction markets can only do sports gambling (the vast majority of their volume) because they self-certify under the CFTC. The CFTC doesn't have the same standards of "insider trading" as the stock market, because insider trading is the entire point of business at the CFTC!

If you're trading, like, oil futures or wheat futures or whatever, you are likely doing so specifically because you have inside information about your business needs or production that you want to hedge.

I understand why people are mad about gambling versus someone who has insider information, but under current US law I'm not sure that there is a case to be made.

在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/16 05:29
> 如果您正在交易石油期货或小麦期货等，您可能会专门这样做，因为您拥有有关您的业务需求的内部信息

在美国，内幕交易在法律上与公平无关，而是与盗窃有关。对冲自己头寸的公司正在将其信息用于自己的目的。一名联邦雇员利用他们所听到的信息进行交易，正在滥用美国人民对他们的信任。

> If you're trading, like, oil futures or wheat futures or whatever, you are likely doing so specifically because you have inside information about your business needs

Insider trading, in the U.S., is not legally about fairness but about theft. A firm hedging its own positions is using its information for its own purposes. A federal employee trading on what they heard is abusing the trust placed in them by the American people.

在 HN 中查看
ikeboy 2026/04/16 07:19
请注意，第二巡回法院以政府信息不属于“财产”为由，驳回了一起内幕交易案件。

参见美国诉 Blaszczak。

这可能是滥用信任，但这并不自动构成犯罪。

Note that the 2nd circuit tossed an insider trading case on government information on the basis that it doesn't count as "property".

See United States v. Blaszczak.

It may be abuse of trust but that's not automatically a crime.

在 HN 中查看
mcmcmc 2026/04/16 04:46
> 预测市场只能进行体育博彩（其大部分交易量），因为它们根据 CFTC 进行自我认证

体育博彩在 11 个州仍然是非法的。由于立法和执法滞后，他们只能做他们正在做的事情。

> CFTC 没有与股票市场相同的“内幕交易”标准，因为内幕交易是 CFTC 的全部业务重点！

在某种程度上确实如此，但使用从公司获得的非公开信息在个人账户上进行交易仍然是非法的。

> Prediction markets can only do sports gambling (the vast majority of their volume) because they self-certify under the CFTC

Sports betting is still illegal in 11 states. They can only do what they’re doing because of legislation and enforcement lag.

> The CFTC doesn't have the same standards of "insider trading" as the stock market, because insider trading is the entire point of business at the CFTC!

True to some extent, but it’s still illegal to use non-public information gained from your firm to trade on personal accounts.

在 HN 中查看
lowkey_ 2026/04/16 02:58
> “如果您在 Kalshi 上进行内幕交易，那么在某些时候这可能而且将会构成联邦犯罪。这是联邦犯罪，”

难道是我理解错了？他似乎总是将两种不同的说法混为一谈。

如果它在某个时候成为联邦犯罪，那么从那时起它就会变得非法——你不能因为人们在犯罪之前所实施的行为而起诉他们。

目前，这可能成为联邦犯罪的唯一方法是政府在不做任何改变的情况下开始根据现有法律对其进行起诉。我认为这不太可能。

> “If you commit insider trading on Kalshi, that can and will at some point be a federal crime. It is a federal crime,”

Am I misunderstanding? It seems like two different statements he always conflates.

If it becomes a federal crime at some point, it will become illegal from that point — you can't prosecute people for acts committed before they were crimes.

The only way that this could be a federal crime right now is if the government starts prosecuting it under existing laws without any changes. I don't see that as likely.

在 HN 中查看
mcmcmc 2026/04/16 04:49
一旦有人因此被定罪，这就是联邦犯罪。在没有任何先例的情况下，将由第一个案例来阐明现有法律如何适用于可能涵盖或不涵盖的新现象，具体取决于法官对论点的看法。
It’s a federal crime once someone gets convicted for it. Without any precedent it will be up to the first case to lay out how existing law applies to new phenomena that may or may not be covered, depending on what a judge thinks of the arguments.

idle_zealot 2026/04/16 03:59
一个理性的反对党会宣布赦免无效。这是对赦免权的有效解释吗？这是否会损害我们法律的合法性？也许吧，但没有那么多 不惩罚 明显而骄傲的罪犯就是这么做的。这就是法治的重点，还记得吗？它创造了合法性，从而创造了稳定性。
A reasonable opposition party would declare the pardons invalid. Is that a valid interpretation of pardon power, does that undermine the legitimacy of our laws? Maybe, but not nearly as much as not punishing obvious and proud criminals does. That's the point of the rule of law, remember? It creates legitimacy, and therefore stability.
在 HN 中查看
hackingonempty 2026/04/16 04:26
> 一个理性的反对党会宣布赦免无效。这是对赦免权的有效解释吗？这是否会损害我们法律的合法性？

不，在美国，赦免权属于总统。 只有宪法修正案才能使赦免无效。

> A reasonable opposition party would declare the pardons invalid. Is that a valid interpretation of pardon power, does that undermine the legitimacy of our laws?

No, in the USA the pardon power belongs to the President. Only a constitutional amendment could invalidate pardons.

在 HN 中查看
idle_zealot 2026/04/16 11:10
只有宪法修正案才能允许行政部门宣战、监管贸易、控制资金或无数其他未列举或明确禁止的权力。但如果违反了这些规则，我们仍然应该遵循行政人员不受惩罚并可以宣布其他任何人宽恕的规则吗？如果是这样的话，实际上只有一条规则：行政为王。你可以用宪法的其余部分擦屁股。
Only a constitutional amendment could allow the executive to declare war, or regulate trade, or control funds, or countless other unenumerated or explicitly disallowed powers. But if those rules are broken, we should still follow the rule that says the executive is immune from punishment and can declare anyone else forgiven? If that's the case there's actually only one rule: the executive is king. You can wipe your ass with the rest of the constitution.
在 HN 中查看
ryandrake 2026/04/16 04:34
正如我们实时发现的那样，总统有权 尝试 做一百万件事，无论合法还是非法，合宪还是违宪，然后无论哪一个没有受到事实上的阻力，就成为实际的权力。往墙上扔东西，如果它粘住了，那就是总统权力。如果不这样做，也没有任何后果。只需耸耸肩，然后向墙上扔其他东西即可。
As we are finding out in real time, the President has the power to try to do a million things, legal or illegal, constitutional or unconstitutional, and then whichever ones don't get pushback defacto become actual powers. Throw something at the wall, if it sticks, then it's a Presidential Power. If it doesn't, there's no consequences. Just shrug and throw something else at the wall.
在 HN 中查看
hackingonempty 2026/04/16 05:01
总统的赦免权已在宪法中列出，并已引起广泛的诉讼。

https://constitution.congress.gov/browse/essay/artII-S2-C1-3...

The President's pardon power is enumerated in the Constitution and has been litigated extensively.

https://constitution.congress.gov/browse/essay/artII-S2-C1-3...

在 HN 中查看
ryandrake 2026/04/16 05:11
这最终并不重要。如果总统试图做某事而没有法庭 实际上 如果他阻止了他，那么无论它是否写入宪法，无论它是否写入法律，或者它过去是否曾被提起诉讼，都无关紧要。他尝试去做，没有人阻止他，所以他能做到。

我们实时发现总统实际上可以做很多事情，只是因为没有人阻止他。

Which ultimately doesn't matter. If the president tries to do something, and no court actually stops him, then it doesn't matter whether or not it's in the Constitution, whether or not it's written down in law, or whether or not it has been litigated in the past. He tried to do it, nobody stopped him, therefore he can do it.

We are finding out in real time that the president can actually do a lot of things, simply because nobody is stopping him.

在 HN 中查看
usefulcat 2026/04/16 05:52
如果你想起诉以前被赦免的人，那么你必须弄清楚如何让法院（即实际起诉的人） 忽略 他们运作的一些最高规则。

我并不是说这永远不会发生，但是a）这听起来确实是一场艰苦的战斗，b）这与总统（一个人）不顾法律为所欲为不是一回事。

If you want to prosecute someone who has previously been pardoned, then you'll have to figure out how to get the courts (that is, the people who would be doing the actual prosecuting) to ignore some of the highest rules by which they operate.

I'm not saying that that could never happen, but a) it sure sounds like an uphill battle and b) it's not the same thing as the president (one person) doing whatever they feel like regardless of the law.

iamnothere 2026/04/16 06:06
我认为这些全面赦免根本不是一件好事，但需要一项修正案来解决它。

我们不知何故养成了一种习惯，当宪法中那些不方便的部分产生问题时，我们就忽略它们，而不是通过列举的过程来修改它们。是的，修宪很难，但这就是重点！

I don’t think these blanket pardons are a good thing at all, but an amendment is needed to fix it.

We have somehow made it a habit to ignore the inconvenient parts of the constitution when they create problems, rather than going through the enumerated process to amend them. Yes, it’s hard to amend the constitution, but that’s the point!

kelnos 2026/04/16 05:38

JumpCrisscross 2026/04/16 06:08
调查和法庭诉讼仍然会产生有价值的公共记录。追捕被赦免的罪犯绝对是下一届政府应该做的事情。 （例如，我们的法庭上先发制人和全面赦免的先例为零。）
There would still be a valuable public record produced by the investigation and court proceedings. Going after pardoned criminals is absolutely something the next administration should do. (We have zero precedent for preëmptive and blanket pardons in our courts, for example.)
在 HN 中查看
hackingonempty 2026/04/16 10:24
单方加兰决定于 1866 年

“宪法赋予总统的赦免权是无限的，弹劾案件除外。它适用于法律已知的每一项罪行，并且可以在赦免后的任何时间行使，无论是在法律诉讼程序进行之前或未决期间，还是在定罪和判决之后。”

没有人会浪费时间去调查无法起诉的犯罪行为。

Ex Parte Garland was decided in 1866

“The power of pardon conferred by the Constitution upon the President is unlimited except in cases of impeachment. It extends to every offence known to the law, and may be exercised at any time after its commission, either before legal proceedings are taken or during their pendency, or after conviction and judgment.”

Nobody is going to waste their time investigating crimes that can't be prosecuted.

idle_zealot 2026/04/16 11:12

You can't pardon violations of State law though. There may be plenty of offenses to go after. Vindictiveness is required at this point.

在 HN 中查看
lowkey_ 2026/04/16 03:15
IANAL 是一个自豪的美国人，哈哈，我们非常明确地不允许根据我们的宪法制定事后法律。这将成为政府滥用权力对付人民的一个巨大途径。

我在您也提到的最初评论中添加的警告是，他们可以尝试找到相关的现有法律并对其进行改造，例如一般证券法，并说这是一个证券市场，所以这一直是非法的——但这不太可能，我怀疑它会起作用。我们更有可能就此通过明确的法律。

IANAL but a proud American haha, and we're very specific on not allowing ex post facto laws per our constitution. It would be a huge avenue for abuse of power by the government against the people.

The caveat I added to my initial comment that you also mentioned was that they could try to find a relevant existing law and retrofit it, e.g. general securities laws, and say that this is a securities market and so this has always been illegal — but it's very unlikely and I doubt it would work. Far, far more likely that we pass explicit laws about this.

在 HN 中查看
chris_money202 2026/04/16 03:18
行政部门根据自己对法律的解释执行法律（起诉犯罪）。由最高法院来决定这种解释是否可以接受或公平。 IE。当犯罪发生时，您可能会根据行政机关对现行法律的解释受到起诉，如果法院认为可以接受，陪审团可能会判定您有罪。 （在美国）
The executive branch executes laws (prosecutes crime) under its own interpretation of the law. It's the supreme court that determines if that interpretation is acceptable or fair. I.e. you could be prosecuted under an executive's interpretation of an existing law when the crime was committed and if the court deems it acceptable you could be found guilty by a jury. (In the USA)

在 HN 中查看
Sol- 2026/04/16 03:47
我知道他想逃避他的平台的责任，但我想我必须承认这似乎是一种合法的辩护。即使可能发生内幕交易，我们也允许股票市场存在，而且（我认为？）纳斯达克或纽约证券交易所没有责任追求这一点。我们对此有一套法律体系。

我认为与传统股票市场相比，预测市场是否会导致过多的犯罪活动和内幕交易，因此仍然存在争论，因此需要出于务实的原因进行限制（即法律体系无法跟上），但这是一个不同的讨论。

I understand he wants to deflect liability from his platform, but I guess I have to concede that it seems like a legitimate defense. We allow the stock market to exist even though insider trading can happen and it's (I think?) not Nasdaq's or NYSE's responsibility to pursue that. We have a legal system for that.

I think there is still the debate to be had whether prediction market enable too much criminal activity and insider trading compared to traditional stock markets and therefore need to be limited for pragmatic reasons (i.e. the legal system can't keep up), but that's a different discussion.

小唐纳德·特朗普是卡尔希的“战略顾问”，也是 Polymarket 的顾问委员会成员。美联储主席候选人凯文·沃什 (Kevin Warsh) 持有 Polymarket 的股份。美国商品期货交易委员会 (CFTC) 主席迈克尔·塞利格 (Michael Selig) 是特朗普任命的，他主张建立预测市场，并希望将 Polymarket 的首席执行官纳入顾问委员会。为特朗普工作的说客戴维·厄本 (David Urban) 受聘于 Polymarket。

如果 不会有任何起诉，但不会是政府内部或与政府有关的任何人。即使这些团体没有在一起，但政府显然一直在从事内幕交易，而司法部对此没有采取任何行动，这一事实清楚地表明，他们将来也不会发生任何事情。金库的门开着，他们随意地走出去，公众的钱从他们的口袋里掉出来。

Donald Trump Jr. is a "strategic advisor" to Kalshi, and is on the advisory board of Polymarket. Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh holds a stake in Polymarket. CFTC Chairman Michael Selig, a Trump appointee, has advocated for prediction markets, and wants to put the CEO of Polymarket on an advisory committee. David Urban, a lobbyist who worked for Trump, was hired by Polymarket.

If there are any prosecutions, it won't be anyone in or connected to the administration. Even if these groups weren't in bed together, the fact that the administration has obviously been engaging in insider trading, and the DoJ hasn't done anything about it, makes it clear nothing will happen to them in the future either. The door to the vault is open and they're casually walking out with the public's money falling out their pockets.

jmyeet 2026/04/16 04:06
Kalshi 的首席执行官应该将他们相当多的财富投入到 Kalshi 身上，押注这将会发生。我会等待。

我们遇到的情况是，通过选择性起诉来赢得忠诚，而头目却不受最高法院任何法律后果的影响，其中 6 名法官是由该头目任命的。现在赦免已经相当公开地出售了。欺骗政府然后支付一小部分的赦免费用更便宜，这样就可以免除任何罚款或还款。

我敢打赌，有一些较低级别的员工正在从预测市场的内幕信息中获利。也许有些人会成为榜样。我不会屏住呼吸。

但所有大型内幕交易都发生在证券市场，特别是石油期货和 SPY 期货。已经到了没有专业人士完全相信未来石油价格的地步，而实物石油价格与未来价格的差距高达 60 美元/桶。在市场变化消息发布前几分钟，我们已经对 SPY 期货进行了 10 亿美元以上的押注。我们不确定是谁在做这件事，但我猜测是政府最高层干的。

The Kalshi CEO should put their considerable wealth into bets on Kalshi that this will happen. I'll wait.

We have a situation where selective prosecution is used to command loyalty while the ringleader has been immunized from any kind of legal consequences by the Supreme Court, 6 of whom were appointed by said ringleader. Pardons are pretty openly sold now. It's cheaper to rip off the government then pay a fraction for a pardon, erasing any fine or repayment.

I bet there are lower level staffers who are profiting off inside information on prediction markets. Maybe some will be made an example of. I won't hold my breath.

But all the big insider trading is occurring in securities markets, particularly with oil futures and SPY futures. It's reached the point where no professionals trust the futre oil prices at all and and the physical oil prices differ from the future price by as much as $60/barrel. We've had $1b+ bets on SPY futures minutes before market-changing news. We don't know for sure who's doing this but my guess is that it's at the highest levels of the administration.

mcmcmc 2026/04/16 03:07
如果他们一直同意国会永远根据内幕消息交易股票，为什么这次会有什么不同呢？

在 HN 中查看
slg 2026/04/16 03:54
这实际上是一个很好的例子，说明这不是党派问题。 国会股票交易禁令是少数不受党派界限限制的问题之一。 共和党和民主党都提出了禁止这种行为的法案，而且共和党和民主党支持此类努力的人口比例非常相似。

我也不会为国会股票交易辩护。 我认为应该禁止。 但是，关于谁在进行交易/投注、他们具体交易/投注的内容以及这些信息向公众传达的内容，存在一些差异。 随机的工作人员押注美国将在某个特定日期攻击伊朗并立即公开，与国会议员自己在攻击前购买武器制造商的股票（明知购买情况最多 45 天内不会向公众披露）之间存在根本区别。

就像我说的，我反对两者，但证明国会股票交易的合理性比我们在这些预测市场上看到的内幕交易要容易得多。 毕竟，国会并没有打算废除所有内幕交易立法，因此即使他们给自己的余地比我们想要的更多，但这里还是有一些细微差别的。

This is actually a good example of this not being a partisan issue. A congressional stock trading ban is one of the few issues that doesn't break down by party lines. Both Republicans and Democrats have put forward bills to ban it and the percentage of the population in support of such efforts is very similar between Republicans and Democrats.

I'm also not going to defend Congressional stock trading. I think it should be banned. But there are some differences about who is doing the trading/betting, what specifically they are trading/betting on, and what that information communicates to the public. There's a fundamental difference between a random staffer betting the US will attack Iran on a certain date which becomes public immediately compared to a Congressperson themselves buying stock in a weapon manufacturer in the leadup to an attack knowing the purchase won't be disclosed to the public for up to 45 days.

Like I said, I would be against both, but it's much easier to justify Congressional stock trading than the insider trading we see on these prediction markets. Afterall, it isn't like Congress is moving to remove all insider trading legislation, so there is some recognition of nuance here even if they give themselves more leeway than we would want.

tptacek 2026/04/16 02:28

在 HN 中查看
mbesto 2026/04/16 05:13
正确的。这是一个重要的区别。

马特·莱文对此有很好的看法。他的非正式、精炼的定义本质上是：

- 内部人士知道一些重要信息而公众不知道，这之间存在着时间差距。

- 有权访问该重要非公开信息的人，不应该将其用于个人利益，但无论如何都会对其进行交易。

- 法院将这种行为视为针对信息较少的交易对手和信息合法“所有者”的欺骗性计划。

换句话说，您通过牺牲他人的利益（例如偷窃）来获利，因为您拥有信息而其他人没有。

两种情况：

(1) 一名美国海军军官在罢工发生前 24 小时就知道了，并与不了解罢工的人下注。

(2) 美国海军军官在袭击发生前 24 小时都不知道袭击，不了解袭击的人也不会采取任何行动。

情况 1 是（或应该是）非法的，因为该官员正在利用这些信息谋取个人利益，而这些信息是出于国防原因而明确提供给他们的（从而违反了合法所有者条款）。

Correct. This is an important distinction.

Matt Levine has a good take on this. His informal, distilled definition is essentially:

- There is a time gap where insiders know something material and the public does not.

- Someone with access to that material nonpublic information, who is not supposed to use it for personal gain, trades on it anyway.

- That conduct is treated by courts as a deceptive scheme against the less‑informed trading counterparty and against the information’s rightful “owner.”

In other words - you profit at another person's expense (e.g. stealing) because you have information and the other person doesn't.

Two scenarios:

(1) a US Naval Officer knows about a strike 24 hours before it happens and places a bet against someone who doesn't have knowledge about the strike.

(2) Neither a US Naval Officer knows about a strike 24 hours before it happens and someone who doesn't have knowledge about the strike do nothing.

Scenario 1 is (or should be) illegal because the officer is using the information for personal gain, when the information was explicitly given to them for national defense reasons (thus violating the rightful owner clause).

在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 05:21
第一种情况是非法的，因为它严重违反了军事保密法。
Scenario 1 is illegal because it gravely violates military secrecy laws.

chao- 2026/04/16 02:47
如果人们只关心预测的准确性（即抛开对社会或公平的价值判断），那么“内幕交易”似乎确实应该使预测市场更加准确。
If all one cares about is the accuracy of predictions (i.e. setting aside value judgements vis-a-vis society or fairness), it does seem like "insider trading" should make prediction markets more accurate.
在 HN 中查看
notahacker 2026/04/16 03:13
这使得它们更加准确 当内幕人士下注时。但为了最大化利润，他们被激励去误导 前 他们下注（更糟糕的是，从社会的角度来看，他们被激励采取反直觉或完全有害的行为来获利，而那些下注的人则认为他们这样做毫无意义）
It makes them more accurate at the time the insider places the bet. But to maximise profit, they are incentivised to misdirect before they place the bet (and worse still from society's perspective, are incentivised to take counter-intuitive or downright harmful actions to profit at the expense of people who bet on the view that it made no sense for them to do that)
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/04/16 03:15
这项贸易如何运作？没有人知道谁是内部人士，他们只看到市场方向。
How does this trade work? Nobody knows who the insider is, they only see market direction.
在 HN 中查看
notahacker 2026/04/16 03:18
您是所投注实体（例如运动队或政府）的发言人或决策者。你公开表明你将做一件事（可能是最有意义的事情），因此市场将其纳入他们的假设中，并为押注做另一件事提供了有利的赔率。然后在做其他事情之前不久就下注。

（确实如此 低级 内部人士对实际结果的影响可能有限，但目前对预测市场的怀疑是，一些参与者确实有这种影响力，或者正在受到有能力的人的有意帮助）

You are a spokesperson or decision maker for an entity which is bet on, like a sports team or the government. You publicly indicate that you are going to do one thing (probably the thing that makes most sense), so the market bakes that into their assumptions and offers favourable odds for bets on doing the other thing. Then you place your bet shortly before you do the other thing.

(it's true that low level insiders might have limited influence on the actual outcome, but the current suspicion with prediction markets is that some of the participants do have that influence, or are being intentionally helped by people that can)

在 HN 中查看
mbesto 2026/04/16 05:15
这涉及博弈论，并且不遵循您建议的预定流程。

这有点像玩扑克……刚刚全押的人是否有口袋A？

This gets into game theory and doesn't follow a predetermined flow like you're suggesting.

It's a bit like playing poker...does the guy who just went all in have pocket Aces or not?

在 HN 中查看
notahacker 2026/04/16 08:11
它真的一点也不像扑克，而且很像我刚才描述的预定流程。预测市场将政治家、运动员或商人的行为视为他们对国家/球队/企业更广泛目标的指示，而不是试图影响他们的投注行为的虚张声势。如果一家公司说他们计划今年首次公开募股，每个人都会合理地认为这意味着董事会希望进行首次公开​​募股，并不是说他们已经决定反对，而是认为他们可以通过匿名方式在 Polymarket 上押注反对首次公开募股，从而赚取相当多的闲置现金
It really isn't at all like poker and really is a lot like the predetermined flow I'm just describing. Prediction markets regard the actions of politicians, sportspeople or businesspeople as indications of their wider goals for the country/team/business, not bluffs to try to influence their betting action. If a company says they're planning to IPO this year, everybody reasonably assumes that means the board want to make the IPO happen, not that they've already decided against it but reckon they can make a decent amount of spare cash anonymously betting against it happening on Polymarket
在 HN 中查看
foltik 2026/04/16 06:25
当一个玩家决定结果时，这并不是真正的游戏。在扑克中，全押的人不能偷看你的牌并在最后一秒换掉他的手牌。
It’s not really a game when one player gets to decide the outcome. In poker, the guy who went all in can’t peek at your cards and swap out his hand at the last second.

在 HN 中查看
Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 02:38
与许多赌博网站不同，预测市场创建了一个赔率市场。没有赌场像某些赌场或 DraftKings 那样占据位置。做市商提供“是”和“否”的份额，而投注者则相互或向做市商买卖这些赔率。
Prediction Markets, unlike many gambling sites, create a marketplace for odds. There's no house taking positions like in certain casinos or on DraftKings. Market makers offer shares in Yes and No while bettors buy and sell these odds to each other or to market makers.

maplant 2026/04/16 02:59
我不太确定你采取什么立场，但采取耙子肯定会改变一项活动的合法性；在加利福尼亚州，私下玩扑克被认为是一种社交游戏，并且是合法的，除非一方收取佣金。
I'm not really sure what position you're taking but taking a rake can certainly change the legality of an activity; in California, playing poker in private is considered a social game and is legal, _except_ if party is taking a rake.

在 HN 中查看
Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 02:58
呃，不是。赌场抽取分成的赌博被视为商业赌博的一种形式，通常被定为非法或有适用的规定。

如果您问为什么预测市场受 CFTC 管辖，这是在积极发展，但通常预测市场被认为受 CFTC 管辖，因为它们可用于对冲事件。

例如，如果您试图在阿曼做生意，但担心伊朗紧张局势蔓延，您可以对伊朗冲突赌注采取“是”立场作为对冲。你可能会失去生意，但可以通过对冲来弥补一些。

“赌博”这个概念在法律上非常复杂，有很多需要理解的地方，所以我建议做一些搜索或法学硕士询问你是否想要哲学定义或法律景观的介绍。

Er it's not. Gambling where the house takes a cut is considered a form of commercial gambling and is often made illegal or has stipulations applied to it.

If you're asking about why prediction markets fall under the CFTC, this is actively evolving but generally prediction markets are considered to be under the CFTC because they can be used to hedge against events.

For example if you're trying to do business in Oman but you're worried about Iran tensions spilling over, you could take a Yes position on an Iran conflict bet as a hedge. You may lose business but can make some of it up in the hedge.

"Gambling" the concept legally is very complicated and has a lot to be understood, so I'd suggest doing some searching or LLM asking if you want an intro on philosophical definitions or the legal landscape.

Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 04:04
我其实没有定义赌博？我只是说这很复杂。我刚才提到，如果赌场抽成，这是一种受监管的商业赌博形式，与社交赌博不同。

有一系列立法和法律先例实际上定义了赌博以及赌博与市场的区别。

I didn't actually define gambling? I just said it's complicated. I just mentioned that if a house takes a cut this is a form of regulated commercial gambling, distinct from social gambling.

There's a body of legislation and legal precedent that actually defines gambling and how that's distinct from markets.

在 HN 中查看
alephnerd 2026/04/16 02:29
> 预测市场公开私人知识的观点难道不是为解释为什么不应将其视为非法赌博而辩护的重要组成部分吗？

不。他们的辩护是，他们是期货合约的游戏化平台，因此应该受到 CFTC 的监管。

美国商品期货交易委员会 还 打击内幕交易，但他们需要时间来制定法规以赶上预测市场。

现在这是一个优先事项 [0]，他们刚刚启动了一项付费举报人 [1] 计划，专门用于捕捉预测市场中的内幕交易者。

[0]- https://www.lw.com/en/insights/new-cftc-enforcement-director...

[1]- https://www.whistleblower.gov/whistleblower-alerts/Insider_T...

> isn't the idea that prediction markets surface private knowledge a big part of the defense as to why they shouldn't be treated as illegal gambling

No. Their defense is that they are a gamified platform for futures contracts and hence should fall under CFTC regulation.

The CFTC also cracks down on insider trading, but it took time for them to write regulations to catch up with prediction markets.

It is now a priority [0] and they have just started a paid whistleblower [1] programs specifically to catch insider traders within prediction markets.

[0] - https://www.lw.com/en/insights/new-cftc-enforcement-director...

[1] - https://www.whistleblower.gov/whistleblower-alerts/Insider_T...

在 HN 中查看
ssharp 2026/04/16 02:37
目前在 Polymarket 主页上，特色市场之一是比特币在未来 5 分钟内会上涨还是下跌。很难证明这比非法赌博更合理。

我发现预测市场在两个方面很有趣：

1）他们喜欢一种非常好的方法来确定某件事发生的概率，这对于选举等事件很有趣

2) 它为聪明的投注者提供了利用和提高技能的途径，而他们在传统体育博彩中受到严格限制或禁止

然而，市场和消费者行为的所有激励结构似乎都会引导这些事情走向堕落的赌博。

On the Polymarket homepage right now, one of the featured markets is whether or not Bitcoin will be up or down over the next 5 minutes. It's hard to justify that as anything more than illegal gambling.

I find prediction markets to be interesting on two fronts:

1) They like a really good way to determine the probability of something happening, which is interesting for events like elections

2) It provides an avenue for smart bettors to take advantage and sharpen their skill, whereas they get severely limited or banned from traditional sports books

However, it seems like all incentive structures for the markets and consumer behavior will steer these things to degenerate gambling.

在 HN 中查看
Karrot_Kream 2026/04/16 02:42
Polymarket 不受 CFTC 监管，在美国被视为非法。 CFTC 不允许对证券价格进行投注。加密货币有点灰色地带，因为它不被视为注册证券。

注意：在这个网站上谈论金融和监管问题变得有点令人沮丧，因为知识水平通常是“我在社交媒体上阅读了一些有关市场的文章”的水平。

Polymarket is not CFTC regulated, it's considered illegal in the US. CFTC does not allow betting on securities prices. Cryptocurrency is a bit of a gray area because it's not considered a registered security.

N.B. it becomes a bit frustrating to talk about financial and regulatory things on this site because the level of knowledge is generally "I read some articles on social media about markets" level.

alephnerd 2026/04/16 02:58

Stuff is changing rapidly so it's best to keep an experienced regulatory lawyer on retainer.

在 HN 中查看
cortesoft 2026/04/16 03:58
我同意这个国家腐败猖獗，但我不同意美国司法部应该在这些市场上追查内幕交易。

为什么我的税款应该花在帮助博彩公司经营博彩业务上？金融市场的内幕交易法和起诉是有意义的，因为公平公正的执法有助于我们整个经济的发展，并允许资本投资于生产性企业。

这些预测市场并非如此。它们不是生产性企业的投资来源，这纯粹是赌博。

为什么美国政府要花钱确保人们可以赌博？如果人们希望赌博公平，他们可以支付自己的执法费用。

我喜欢内幕交易在这些平台上猖獗的想法。一旦人们意识到赌注的另一方提前知道结果，也许他们就会停止在这些平台上赌注，然后就死掉了。

I agree the country has rampant corruption, but I disagree that the US DOJ should be pursuing insider trading on these markets.

Why should my tax dollars be spent on helping a bookie run their gambling business? Insider trading laws and prosecution in the financial markets makes sense, because the fair and impartial enforcement helps all of our economy, and allows capital to do its job investing in productive ventures.

These prediction markets are not that. They aren’t sources of investment for a productive enterprise, it is pure gambling.

Why should the US government spend money making sure people can gamble? If people want their gambling to be fair, they can pay for their own enforcement.

I like the idea of insider trading running rampant on these platforms. Once people realize that the other side of their bet knows the outcomes in advance, maybe they will stop betting their money on these platforms and they can just die.

JohnMakin 2026/04/16 04:28
> 为什么我的税款应该花在帮助博彩公司经营博彩业务上？

我的意思是，现行法律正是这样做的。美国联邦调查局（FBI）已经对主要体育博彩网站进行了与玩家投掷或操纵比赛等相关的调查。如果您在体育博彩上犯下欺诈行为，您绝对可以入狱。

> Why should my tax dollars be spent on helping a bookie run their gambling business?

I mean, current laws do exactly this. The FBI has done investigations for major sports betting sites related to players throwing or fixing games, etc. If you commit fraud on a sportsbook you absolutely can go to jail.
